title: rubus subintegrifolius
intro: Rubus subintegrifolius, also known as the Himalayan Raspberry or Himalayan Bramble, is a type of thorny shrub that belongs to the rose family, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits and medicinal uses. Rubus subintegrifolius has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Rubus subintegrifolius can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
